meta.title: Donnie Darko — Illustrated Playthrough
meta.source: Calibrated against the live site (http://localhost:8080). Narrative cross-referenced with src/aid/aid1.html … aid9.html. See steps/donnie.guide.steps.json for the original 43-step guide.
meta.notes: Canvas-local coords (authored stage 0..800 x 0..500; rendered at 768x480, mapper scales). The intro montage and several screens animate perpetually (scanlines / a live-ticking counter), so those steps use mode:fixed — diff-stable can't settle on a never-static canvas. Static end-states use diff-stable.

steps.caption: The site opens on a Win98 pop-up cycling through distorted portraits, with a red bullseye target. Click the bullseye repeatedly to advance the intro montage.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-05-30. Bullseye is a fixed authored target at (478,250); each click advances one frame. ~3 clicks reach the proceed screen; the 4th is a harmless no-op. Montage scanlines animate constantly → mode:fixed.

steps.caption: The montage settles on “The Tangent Universe collapsed 8981 days … ago.” Click the red “.proceed.” link.
steps.note: CALIBRATED. The seconds counter ticks live, so this screen never diff-settles → mode:fixed. .proceed. link at ~(658,280).

steps.caption: A pop-up offers “if you have been here before, enter the Level 1 password” with a text field, or “otherwise proceed here”. As a first-time visitor, click “otherwise proceed here”. The screen then forwards automatically, assembling an animated text collage that resolves into a scanned detention-centre report on DARKO, DONALD J.
steps.note: CALIBRATED. The Level-1 password (breathe) cheat would go in the field at ~(310,205). The collage auto-forwards (~40s) to the static document (matches aid no2a.jpg). minWaitMs floors the wait so diff-stable can't latch onto the brief static gap before the collage builds.

steps.caption: Click the detention report to open the terminal affidavit: “AFFIDAVIT FOR WARRANT OF ARREST AND DETENTION … OFFENSE OF: ATTEMPTED ESCAPE - CLASS A”, ending with the prompt “C:>MORE ? (Y/N)”.
steps.note: CALIBRATED. Green terminal text (blinking cursor) → mode:fixed.

steps.caption: Type Y at the “C:>MORE ? (Y/N)” prompt to read the arresting officer's statement (Donnie found asleep in a field, sleepwalking), which ends with another “C:>MORE ? (Y/N)”.
steps.note: CALIBRATED. Keyboard reaches Ruffle via focused player. N does nothing in the original; Y advances.

steps.caption: Type Y again. The report dissolves into the Level 1 main menu — a cloudscape with a lone figure and several red crosshair hotspots labelled “something”, “close”, “I”, “want”…
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-05-30. Reaches the menu (matches aid no2c.jpg). Clouds drift subtly → looser threshold.

steps.caption: On the cloudscape menu, the drifting words are decoration; the three fixed red crosshairs are the level selectors. Hover the lower-centre crosshair (Level 1) and click — the screen tears apart into “THINGS AREN'T THAT SIMPLE!”, beginning Level 1.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-05-30, reproducible 2/2 (23% diff). clouds.swf button 0092 'level 1 is active' fires an internal go→_level2 (loads book.swf), so it works; levels 2 & 3 are gated until the prior level is completed. Crosshair must be clicked precisely (red-detected centre); hover initiates the rollover.

steps.caption: A thin red line leads up to the top-left. Click the first circle there — a Middlesex Times Dispatch obituary for Roberta Sparrow opens in a new browser tab. Note the surname (“a bird”), then close the tab to continue.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-05-30. Opens news/pop1.html in a NEW TAB; the runner screenshots then closes it (closing continues the game). Roberta Sparrow obituary → password 'her name was that of a bird' = sparrow.

steps.caption: A second circle further down the red line opens a second obituary (Dr Kenneth Monnitoff) in a new tab. Read it and close it; a third circle then appears.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-05-30. Opens news/pop2.html in a NEW TAB. After both obituaries a 3rd red crosshair appears at (490,151).

steps.caption: After both obituaries, a third red circle appears at upper-centre over the skeletal figure (“a guide in a time of great danger”). Click it: a banner unfurls reading “her name was that of a bird *******” — a seven-letter password prompt. The obituary named her Roberta Sparrow, so the answer is the bird: sparrow.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-05-31. Red-detected 3rd crosshair at canvas (490,151). Reveals the 'her name was that of a bird *******' hint (book.swf, readonly TextField9). The seven asterisks are a STATIC hint, not a live field — they never change. Banner text animates in → mode:fixed.

steps.caption: In the small password field below the hint, type the answer — sparrow — and press Enter. The banner reshapes into a letter: Karen P. Monnitoff (Library of Congress) forwarding “The Philosophy of Time Travel” by Roberta Sparrow to Elizabeth, with instructions to hide it where it can never be found.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-05-31. The pass field (book.swf EditText id 0120, password) sits inside the 'box' clip placed at root (211.95,209), field-local (1,4.25) → stage ~(213,213). Click-to-focus at (235,221) — earlier clicks at y≈155-185 hit the readonly hint ABOVE the field and never focused it. A per-frame loop checks `this.pass eq "sparrow"` and, on match, gotoAndStop(3)+_root.spear.gotoAndPlay → Monnitoff letter (matches aid l213.jpg). Typing does NOT clear the asterisk hint (separate field); Enter commits. Reaching this in a fresh full run leaves the field empty, so no pre-clear needed.

steps.caption: Click anywhere on the letter to continue. The page clears to white and a single red line of text fades in at lower-right: “DO YOU BELIEVE IN TIME TRAVEL ?”.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-05-31. Click on the letter body advances book.swf to the question screen (matches aid l214.jpg). The red question sits at lower-right, roughly canvas (590-775, 310).

steps.caption: Click the red “DO YOU BELIEVE IN TIME TRAVEL ?” question. The site navigates into the Tangent area: a dark room where the shadowy figure of Frank looms behind three Win98 pop-up windows spelling out a phrase — “remember”, “one”, “word”.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-05-31; stopOnNav added 2026-06-03. Red-detected question text at canvas (646,311). This click NAVIGATES via a getURL chain: book.swf → /the/index.html → (meta-refresh) → /the/tangent/index.html (smurf.swf: the 'remember / one / word' windows). The question fades in, so we tap up to 5×, but `stopOnNav:true` HALTS the repeat the instant the first tap navigates — WITHOUT it the extra taps land on the smurf page and blow it through to universe/is_unstable.html, skipping steps 14–15. NAVIGATION BOUNDARY: next steps run on smurf.swf in the live flow; do NOT `navigate` (the SWF is inert standalone). Overlays are re-suppressed after the nav.

steps.caption: Three Win98 pop-up windows are open, spelling the phrase “remember / one / word”. Close the first window (“remember”) using the X in its title bar. All three windows close together and a new pop-up appears, lower-right: the shadowy figure of Frank with a single word — “smurf”. Remember it.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-05-31 (probe-l1-xhair3 --tangent + owner's screen recording). smurf.swf has ONE Stop at the 3-window frame (39): clicking ANY window's X fires button 0013 (this._visible=0; this.gotoAndPlay(12); _root.play()), and frame 40 REMOVEs all three windows at once → builds the Frank window (JPEG id 0023) + 'smurf' text (id 0025) → Stop at frame 48. So a single close reveals Frank; there is no intermediate 'only window 3 left' state in this rebuild. X of the 'remember' window = title-bar close button at canvas (212,262) (rightmost of the _ □ X cluster; the window spans ~x30-225). Frank window animates in → mode:fixed.

steps.caption: Close the Frank pop-up (“smurf”) using the X in its title bar. The screen tears into a red fracture and the site navigates into the Tangent Universe — a destabilised mirror world (philosophy.swf), with the figure suspended at the top of the frame.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-05-31. The Frank window sits lower-right (~x562-787); its CLOSE X is at canvas (775,262) — ~13px left of the right border, the rightmost of the _ □ X cluster. NB earlier clicks at x≈745-758 hit the minimize/maximize buttons (no-op) — the X is further right at ~775. Closing it fires _root.play() → frames 49-54 → GetUrl 'universe/is_unstable.html'. NAVIGATION BOUNDARY: this loads a fresh SWF (philosophy.swf); the runner waits for the new Ruffle + re-suppresses the hw-accel overlay automatically. Per owner: on is_unstable.html the next Win98 window relocates to the TOP-RIGHT at the same 800×500 canvas. Red transition animates → mode:fixed (6s).

steps.caption: The Tangent Universe loads (philosophy.swf): the figure hangs at the top of the frame over a red fracture, and a tiny red crosshair sits at the far-left edge of the stage. Click it — the first Win98 pop-up window slides into the lower-left.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-01 (probe-grid-book). philosophy.swf autoplays a ~5.5s intro on load, so delayBeforeMs 9000 lets the far-left red diamond crosshair appear. Hit-area is the tiny diamond at the very edge — (45,330) MISSES; (12,330) hits. This click → _root.gotoAndStop/play('pop1') which click-steps the windows (NOT auto-played): each subsequent click summons the NEXT window (dad → donnie → straight → gran-donnie, one sub-swf loads per click). Scan-lined windows animate → mode:fixed.

steps.caption: Click the middle of the first window. A second Win98 window opens to the lower-right.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-01. Click the just-opened window's middle to advance to the next (loads donnie.swf). Windows rearrange leftward each step (owner: keep clicking each newest window's middle).

steps.caption: Click the middle of the second window. A third window opens.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-01. The right window's true middle is y≈350 (earlier y≈375-385 missed). Loads straight.swf.

steps.caption: Click the middle of the third window. The windows resolve into a 2×2 grid of four, with the grandmother/Donnie figure (gran-donnie) filling in behind them.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-01. Loads gran-donnie.swf — which ALSO reveals the page's transparent `[data-popup="gran-donnie.swf"]` HTML overlay (right:0, 60% width, z-index 999) that now covers the whole canvas. The grid keeps re-loading gran-donnie every frame (idle loop) until the hidden advance button is clicked next. Grid forms slowly → fixed 9000.

steps.caption: Click the bottom-right of the grid. The four windows take on “Microsoft Internet” title bars, then the scene dissolves and the Philosophy of Time Travel book builds itself from scattered words — finally a pop-up appears: the shadow of Frank asking “you found me… remember the word?”, with a password field.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-01. THE ADVANCE = hidden button id 0104 (`_root.gotoAndStop('words')`), placed on the main timeline (frame 108) only after the 4th window; its hit-rect is canvas x[258-435] y[290-467], centre (346,378). REQUIRES `neutralizeOverlays` — the gran-donnie span (above) sits at the SAME viewport region as the canvas (x512-1280) and eats the click otherwise. The book opens with a ~12s intro (words → book → Frank slides in) → fixed 14000 lands on Frank's window.

steps.caption: Type the word from Frank — smurf — into the field. It commits live: the screen fills with four Win98 windows each showing a Smurf, and red chapter crosshairs scatter down the left edge.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-01. Field = EditText id 0165 (`pass`, password); a per-frame check `if (pass eq 'smurf') gotoAndPlay('smurf')` commits — NO Enter. `locateField` finds Frank's navy titlebar at runtime and types into its centre + ~186px (the window repositions between runs, so don't hard-code). Focus is automatic (clicking focuses <ruffle-player>); the ONLY trick is hitting the editable rect (centre, not the bottom edge). Smurf windows carry grey '@.' titlebars (NOT navy) so they don't confuse titlebar detection.

steps.caption: Donnie’s letter to Roberta Sparrow lies underneath the Smurf grid — clicking a Smurf window flashes the grid hidden for under half a second, exposing the text. One word is red: “breathe” (“…I can breathe a sigh of relief…”). Click it to reveal the prize: “the word you just saw is your password to level 2.” Level 1 is complete; breathe is the Level 2 password.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-01. The letter (red word `breathe` ≈ canvas (433,406)) sits UNDER the Smurf grid; clicking any Smurf window (button 0176) flashes the grid hidden <0.5s. Flash with the TOP-RIGHT window (620,120), NOT the bottom-left — breathe overlaps the bottom-left window, so flashing it would just re-hit it. Fixed burst of 8 taps @~0.11s (owner's tip) to beat the sub-second flash; breathe usually registers in 1-2 taps and the rest hit empty space harmlessly (verified: 6 extra taps cause no harm — flash(620,120) is empty once the grid clears and target(433,406) is over the book, not a button). NB do NOT gate on `until:navytitlebar` here — the Smurf grid windows ALSO carry navy 'Microsoft Internet' titlebars, so that stop condition false-fires. The advance is an INTERNAL SWF transition (URL unchanged) → no awaitNav. NEXT FRONTIER: the `level2.swf` HTML overlay (`<a href="/menu.html">`, revealed ~12s after the book) is the Level 2 gateway; Level 2's own puzzles are not yet calibrated. Closing the Roberta window instead lets you browse the book chapters (Level 3 clue = `rose`).

steps.section: Level 1 — The Philosophy of Time Travel
steps.caption: Level 1 hides more than a password gate — Roberta Sparrow's lost book, “The Philosophy of Time Travel,” lives here too. Small red chapter crosshairs scatter down the dark left edge of the stage (their positions shift each visit); click one and the book opens on the page to the right — here its title page, by “Roberta Ann Sparrow,” 1944. (The scene renders almost black in-game; brightened here so it reads.)
steps.note: OPTIONAL narrative (added 2026-06-03, owner-requested). The chapter crosshairs are small (~2-5px) red dots at RANDOM positions on the LEFT — detect at runtime, never hardcode (fallback (60,150) if none found). `maxPixels:20` keeps the detector off the big persistent red zigzag (~60px, lower-left). Owner confirmed the crosshairs stay visible whether or not the 'password to level 2' window is open, so we do NOT close it. The book page (right) renders VERY dark — `brighten:3` triples the captured frame's RGB so the title page / chapter text is legible in the walkthrough (game render unchanged). neutralizeOverlays guards against a stray click hitting the level2.swf `<a href="/menu.html">` overlay (right side). Validated via src/test-chapters.js (drives tu-* through breathe → detectClick left-band dots → book renders). First click typically opens to the title page ("THE PHILOSOPHY OF TIME TRAVEL — Roberta Sparrow").

steps.section: Level 1 — The Philosophy of Time Travel
steps.caption: Click another crosshair to turn the page. The Foreword warns: “I pray that this book is merely a work of fiction. If it is not, then I pray for you, the reader of this book.” Its chapters read like a blueprint of the film — the Tangent Universe (“highly unstable, sustaining itself for no longer than several weeks”), the Artifact that falls from the sky, the Living Receiver chosen to return it (Donnie, “tormented by terrifying dreams, visions and auditory hallucinations”), and the Manipulated Living who “will do anything to save themselves from Oblivion.”
steps.note: OPTIONAL narrative (added 2026-06-03). Second chapter beat — default (largest) pick lands on the dot that SPAWNED from the first click (the fresh dot grows largest), turning to a different page (clicking the SAME dot just re-shows its page). `brighten:3` for legibility. Which chapter lands is somewhat run-random; the caption covers the book generally. See tu-chapter-1's note for the mechanic. l2-enter navigates to the golf section next, so this detour is informational + safe.

steps.section: Level 1 — The Philosophy of Time Travel
steps.caption: The complete book, page by page. There are ten chapter crosshairs in all — hover one and its name appears; click it and that page unfurls. Captured here in full (and brightened — the game renders them almost black): the title page, the Foreword, seven chapters, both appendices, and the handwritten names of the Manipulated.
steps.note: CURATED GALLERY (2026-06-03). NOT live-driven: auto-clicking all ten random, dynamic crosshairs is unreliable (see HANDOVER), so these pages were captured in an owner-driven recorder session (src/record-chapters.js — the owner clicked each crosshair; every click saved a brightened frame; the owner DRAGGED Frank's password window aside by its title bar, which is possible because the Win98 windows are draggable). Files + titles live in assets/chapters/index.json; the runner passes this step straight to the manifest (no browser action) and the generator embeds the pages as a captioned grid. To re-capture: node src/record-chapters.js, play to the crosshairs, click each; pick the settled frames into assets/chapters/.

steps.caption: Level 1's reward word — breathe — unlocks Level 2. (In live play the now-active Level 2 crosshair on /menu.html opens a Win98 password modal; typing breathe opens the “sleep golfing” door — a giant red cross — which leads here.) The screen is a golf course at night: a cart bottom-left and the date counter ticking up from 10-02-1988.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-02 (probe-golf.js). main.html autoloads golf.swf (800×500→768×480) via JS — DRIVABLE STANDALONE, so we navigate straight here rather than driving the cross-tab menu→breathe→red-cross entry (the runner can't follow target=_blank popups as the main page). golf.swf does NOT auto-advance; it waits on the course frame for the red-square click next, and preloads draw.swf + birds.swf.

steps.caption: Click the small red square on the course. A news popup (pop3) opens in a new tab and the scene advances.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-02. Red square = button 0091 → getURL news/pop3.html (new-tab popup, captured + closed) + advances golf.swf to frame 'part2'. The advance square is tiny (~4px); red sentence TEXT forms huge clusters — if detecting instead of hard-coding, filter --maxpx 40.

steps.caption: Click the next red square. The scene turns to big red “Wake Up Donnie!” text over figures lying on the green.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-02. Button 0093 → frame 'donnie'. After this the simple red-square advance stops; the next interaction is the 'go _level2' hop into the lifeline puzzle.

steps.caption: Click low-left on the course. golf.swf loads the real puzzle — draw.swf — as a second layer: Jim Cunningham's Lifeline Exercise, a FEAR ⟷ LOVE blackboard with a TV.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-02. Button 0122 'go _level2' → loadMovie draw.swf into _level2 (also triggers love.swf→holder1, fear.swf→holder2, street.swf preload). Clicked ×2 because the button fades in (a single early click can miss); the 2nd click lands harmlessly on the blackboard if the 1st already advanced. draw.swf frame labels: go/in/out/exercise1/fear/love/exercise2…

steps.caption: Click the TV on the blackboard. It starts “Lifeline Exercise #1” — a situation about Juanita and cheating on the math test.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-02. The TV = button 0084 → _root.gotoAndPlay('exercise1'). Question text is pulled from texts.html into the 'revealtext' field.

steps.caption: Mark the X on the FEAR side: click the left half of the bar. A chalk X drops onto the lifeline.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-02. The bar is BINARY, not positional: LEFT half = button 0101 → _root.lifeline='fear'; RIGHT half = 0102 = 'love'. Any click on the correct half passes — exact x doesn't matter. Answer #1 = FEAR (owner-confirmed). Evaluation fires on rollOUT (next step).

steps.caption: Move the cursor off the bar. The exercise evaluates the mark — correct — and advances to “Lifeline Exercise #2” (Ling Ling and the found wallet).
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-02. rollOut on the bar fires lifeline_control.evaluate() → correct/wrong frames (Frank 'bunny' feedback). Moving the pointer away is what triggers it.

steps.caption: Exercise #2 — mark FEAR again: click the left half of the bar.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-02. Answer #2 = FEAR (owner-confirmed; video frame ex_30). Same binary bar (button 0101 = left/fear).

steps.caption: Move off the bar again. Correct — and Donnie rebuts Cunningham: “life isn't that simple… you can't just lump everything into these two categories… the whole spectrum of human emotion.”
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-02. Second evaluate() → Donnie's rebuttal monologue scene.

steps.caption: Click the TV. Donnie's words filter in.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-02. This is the step that loads license.swf (console: 'License file is available'); main.html then arms a 5s timer that reveals the [data-url] exit overlay — so the exit becomes available shortly after here.

steps.caption: Click the TV again. The screen fills with a grid of Win98 windows, each holding one big red letter: WAKE / UP DO / NNIE.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-02. Reaches the 'WAKE UP DONNIE' letter-windows. The story continues IN this tab — click inside a window (next step) for the sleepgolfing payoff (aid beats 28-31), encoded 2026-06-03 from the owner's recorded playthrough (src/record-session.js → output/record-golf.log).

steps.caption: Click inside one of the WAKE UP DONNIE windows. The screen forwards: back on the golf course at night, a figure in blue crawls across the green while the question assembles in flying letters — “do you think he was sleepgolfing?”
steps.note: ENCODED 2026-06-03 from the owner's recorder trail (click (426,406) @+42s → the sleepgolfing scene, capture click-14). Aid step 27→28: 'click inside one of the windows → screen forwards'. The scene then plays ~9s (Donnie crawling + letters assembling) before the white arrow appears — hence the long fixed wait.

steps.caption: Click on Donnie — the figure in blue out on the green. He drops to the grass and crawls, a thin line tracing out from him across the course.
steps.note: ENCODED 2026-06-03 (probe src/test-donnie-click.mjs). Aid step 28's literal instruction: 'click on donnie'. At the FROZEN standing scene (the previous step settles on the freeze — the figures at ~(417,403) blue / (278,382)), clicking the blue figure advances into the crawling sequence, during which the WHITE ARROW slides along the right edge (next step detects it). Clicking during the letter ASSEMBLY instead RESETS the whole sequence to 10-02-1988 — hence the diff-stable freeze-wait before this.

steps.caption: Click the white arrow at the right edge. Donnie wakes on the green and a Win98 window pops the next riddle: “who found the wallet?” The answer is ling ling — though the scene moves on regardless.
steps.note: ENCODED 2026-06-03 (owner trail: click (760,359) @+51s → wallet-question window + arrow, capture click-15). Aid step 29: 'click the white arrow → popup who found the wallet? = ling ling (auto-forwards even if skipped)' — we skip typing, as the owner did. repeat:2 guards the arrow's animation timing.

steps.caption: Click on. The night course gives way to a suburban street as new words stretch across the screen: “i have been waiting for you.”
steps.note: ENCODED 2026-06-03 (owner trail: click (596,308) @+52.8s → 'i have been waiting for you' assembling, capture click-16). The sidewalk scene takes ~7s to settle before the wallet hotspot is ready.

steps.caption: On the sidewalk — “i've been waiting for you” — click the dot. A Win98 window opens the found wallet: Jim Cunningham's ID inside.
steps.note: ENCODED 2026-06-03 (owner trail: click (597,396) @+59.8s → the open-wallet window with Jim's ID, capture click-17). Aid step 30: 'click the o dot → wallet popup'. In the rebuild the wallet is an IN-CANVAS Win98 window, not a browser popup.

steps.caption: Click inside the wallet window. The screen cuts to black and red diagonal lines streak up toward the corner — at the tip of the longest line, a dot blinks.
steps.note: ENCODED 2026-06-03 (owner trail: click (566,206) @+63.5s → the red-lines scene, capture click-18). The lines grow over a few seconds; the blinking dot rides the tip of the longest one (~canvas (498,80) in the owner's run; (475,85) in an earlier probe).

steps.caption: Click the blinking dot at the tip of the longest line. The Cunning Visions news article opens in a new tab — Jim Cunningham's media empire, the thread that leads to the finale. Read it and close it.
steps.note: ENCODED 2026-06-03. The blinking dot's CLICK TARGET in the rebuild is the 44×44 `[data-url]` HTML exit overlay (viewport-positioned right:36%/top:12.95% — its canvas-local spot varies with geometry, so a DOM click is the robust move; the owner's trail confirmed the same region opened pop4 then sparkle). 1st click → /news/pop4.html (the article; runner captures + closes). The 2nd+ click rewrites the href and would open /sparkle/motion/ — that's the next step's navigate. waitVisibleMs polls for the overlay (revealed 5s after license.swf, which loaded back at l2-mono-tv1).

steps.caption: Click the dot once more and the way out opens: the 'Sparkle Motion' finale. The screen here is dark, the date counter ticking — the movie is waiting for you to begin.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-02. In live play the golf page's `[data-url]` overlay (right:36%/top:12.95%/44×44/z999, revealed ~5s after license.swf loads at l2-mono-tv1) opens `/sparkle/motion/main.html` in a NEW TAB. The runner closes popups, so we NAVIGATE here directly instead — it's drivable standalone (`<embed>` phase2_end.swf 800×500, autoplay; canvas bottom-right via `flex items-end justify-end`). phase2_end.swf loads PAUSED (Ruffle gates autoplay on a user gesture) → the next step's click starts it.

steps.caption: Click once to begin. The finale then plays itself: red “burn it down” beams streak up from Donnie, Jim Cunningham's Virginia driver's license surfaces (45a Rose Street — remember “rose”), the Sparkle Motion dancers and BURN / DOWN windows fill the screen, and it auto-forwards back to the main menu. Level 2 complete.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-02 (HEADED — see ⚠). A single LEFT-CLICK starts the paused phase2_end.swf (the aid's 'right-click→play' is just 'start it'; right-click shows no menu — `fscommand showmenu=false`). It then AUTOPLAYS the entire finale with NO further clicks (~22s: beams → Jim's license popup [45a rose st = the Level 3 password] → BURN DOWN p1-p6 fire-popup grid + dancers → auto-forward via getURL to /menu.html). Verified end-to-end via `src/probe-sparkle.js --observe --clickstart 400,250 --headed`: reds bloom bottom-right, spread across the dancers at ~t16s, then url→/menu.html at ~t26s. ⚠️ HEADED ONLY — headless Playwright-Ruffle STALLS on the donnie/beams frame and never advances (tried every click/key, `--resumetest`). So the walkthrough generation run MUST be headed (`cli.js --headed`). `awaitNavMs:30000` lets the runner wait for the auto-forward to /menu.html = end of Level 2. (Golf-tab beats 28-31 — sleepgolfing/wallet `ling ling`/sidewalk/red-lines — are skipped by this exit; documented in HANDOVER as optional TODO.)

steps.caption: Back at the menu, the Level 3 crosshair asks for a password — “the name of the street he lives in… like the flower” = rose (Jim's address, 45a Rose Street). It opens the final section, “from the sky”: a police car and men in suits, a figure lifting a chandelier — “they made me do it” — over a fallen jet engine.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-02. Live entry: menu → Level 3 'o' crosshair → password popup = `rose` → red-cross gateway /from/the/sky/index.html (new tab) → /from/the/sky/main.html (new tab). We navigate directly (drivable standalone: `<embed>` trampolin.swf 185f@30fps 800×500, autoplay; canvas BOTTOM-LEFT via `flex items-end`; mapper adapts). trampolin.swf autoplays a ~1.5s intro then STOPS on the 'they made me do it' scene awaiting the first click.

steps.caption: In the date/location block (center-right) click the location — “Middlesex, Virginia,” the town from Jim's address. A Win98 popup opens with an x-ray of the chandelier.
steps.note: RE-CALIBRATED 2026-06-03 (owner manual-demo recorder, src/record-sky.js → output/record-sky.log). Clicking the 'Middlesex, Virginia' line @ canvas (665,295) loads lamp.swf = the chandelier x-ray popup. The click is FLAKY (sometimes the location hotspot doesn't register), so `clickUntilNet` re-clicks until lamp.swf actually loads over the network (it loadMovie's into the main movie, so the request is visible on the page). Earlier coord (615,285) was slightly low/left. Block reads: date 10-02-1988 / 2310 Powderham Drive / Sarasota Heights / Middlesex, Virginia.

steps.caption: Click the telephone icon in the chandelier popup. The “MISSING PLANE LOSES ENGINE” article (with a spoiler warning) opens in a new tab — read it, then it closes. Back on the scene, the FAA Office of Accident Investigation document now unfurls with a red “>>> LAUNCH THE DOCUMENT” button.
steps.note: RE-CALIBRATED 2026-06-03 (owner manual-demo recorder). The telephone icon is @ canvas (663,244) (NOT the old (610,180), which was the popup interior — it opened pop6 by luck but is off the icon). Clicking it → getURL news/pop6.html (the engine article, NEW TAB; the runner captures + closes it). repeat:2 guards the flaky hotspot. This does NOT itself load the transcript — that's the SEPARATE launch-document click next. (pop6 opens on its own tab, so a network-gate on the main page can't see it; a plain repeat-click is the robust choice here.)

steps.caption: Click the red “>>> LAUNCH THE DOCUMENT” button at the bottom of the scene. The FAA document springs to life and the transcript of an extraordinary phone call begins to play.
steps.note: NEW + CALIBRATED 2026-06-03 (owner manual-demo recorder → output/record-sky.log: click canvas(365,452) → phone.swf loads ~3s later). This is the REAL advance the old steps were missing entirely: the '>>> LAUNCH THE DOCUMENT' button at the BOTTOM of the canvas @ (365,452) loads phone.swf into _level1 = the FAA transcript. `clickUntilNet` re-clicks until phone.swf is requested on the page — robust to the button animating in late AND to the load being flaky. ✅ Works HEADLESS too (the old 'HEADED-only' belief was a wrong-coordinate artifact — the launch button, not the telephone, is the advance).

steps.caption: The transcript reads out the impossible: an exact-replica jet engine — serial numbers and hand-painted spiral matching a 747 that landed safely in San Francisco on October 2, 1988 — before the line goes dead. The engine sinks into red spirals and the screen turns to its last words: “time is up, donnie.” THE END.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-02; reached reliably after the launch-document fix 2026-06-03. After the transcript (phone.swf in _level1, ~2min; text from phone.html/transcript.html — the 'exact-replica engine fell from a plane that landed safely Oct 2 1988' paradox) the screen AUTO-CHANGES to 'time is up, donnie.' (engine + red spirals) = the END of the whole game. No click needed. fixedMs 145000 is a CEILING, not a dwell — settle.js now polls in chunks and returns the last good frame if the page is closed/auto-forwards early (so closing the browser no longer crashes the run). Tune down once the true transcript→ending duration is measured from a clean run.
